questions.question: Scenario: In BigQuery, you are using a dataset for analysis and need to grant access to third-party companies. You aim to minimize data sharing costs and maintain data currency. What solution should you opt for in order to achieve low data sharing costs and ensure current data for third-party companies accessing the dataset in BigQuery?
options: Use Analytics Hub to control data access, and provide third party companies with access to the dataset.
Use Cloud Scheduler to export the data on a regular basis to Cloud Storage, and provide third-party companies with access to the bucket.
Create a separate dataset in BigQuery that contains the relevant data to share, and provide third-party companies with access to the new dataset.
Create a Dataflow job that reads the data in frequent time intervals, and writes it to the relevant BigQuery dataset or Cloud Storage bucket for third-party companies to use.
answer: Use Analytics Hub to control data access, and provide third party companies with access to the dataset.
questions.explanation: A. Use Analytics Hub to control data access, and provide third party companies with access to the dataset.Using Analytics Hub to control data access allows you to manage and govern access to the dataset efficiently. You can set up fine-grained access controls and permissions, ensuring that third-party companies only have access to the data they need. This solution helps keep costs low by minimizing the duplication of data, as all parties can access the same dataset directly.Why the other options are incorrect:B. Using Cloud Scheduler to export data on a regular basis to Cloud Storage and providing access to the bucket may result in higher costs due to data duplication. This approach also may not ensure that the data is current, as there may be delays in exporting and syncing the data.C. Creating a separate dataset in BigQuery for sharing data may lead to increased storage costs and data redundancy. It also requires additional effort to manage and maintain multiple datasets, which can be inefficient.D. Creating a Dataflow job to read and write data at frequent intervals can be resource-intensive and may result in higher costs. This approach may also introduce delays in data availability, as the process of reading and writing data takes time.
